data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1007 GMT - A strong surprise in U.S. nonfarm payrolls data could fuel volatility in gold prices, DHF Capital's Bas Kooijman says in a commentary. The precious metal is trading near record levels as investors stay cautious ahead of the U.S. jobs report that could shape sentiment and monetary-policy expectations, he notes. Recent U.S. data, including weaker private payrolls, have reinforced expectations of a dovish Federal Reserve, with traders nearly certain of a 25bp reduction at the central bank's September meeting, he says. Investors are likely betting on up to three cuts this year, he adds. Spot gold rises 0.2% to around $3,552/oz. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

1000 GMT - Better-than-expected U.K. retail sales data Friday should have limited implications for sterling, Monex Europe analyst say in a note. Retail sales increased 0.6% in July compared with the previous month, above the 0.5% forecast by economists in a WSJ survey. The data aren't indicative of a resurgence in consumer demand and the Office for National Statistics has showed inaccuracies in its monthly calculations, the analysts say. Sterling rises 0.3% to $1.3478. The euro trades flat at 0.8668 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0953 GMT - Thailand's parliament has picked a new prime minister, but political uncertainty is set to persist. The new PM, Anutin Charnvirakul, leader of the center-right Bhumjaithai Party, won with backing from the opposition People's Party on the condition to dissolve parliament within four months. This suggests snap elections could be held in early 2026 but uncertainty will likely continue, Nomura analysts say. They see limited signs of a turnaround in economic conditions, with political unrest dimming growth prospects. If political issues delay government expenditure, that will drag on markets and the economy. For now, Thai markets have taken the news in their stride, with the benchmark SET index edging down a touch but still up 0.8% at 1262.72. The baht is also little moved, lingering around 32 to the dollar. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0944 GMT - China likely continued its export resilience in August despite a slowdown in growth, according to a poll of economists by The Wall Street Journal. Outbound shipments likely rose 5.2% on year last month, moderating from the 7.2% increase in July, the poll showed. Import growth is expected to have slowed to 1.8% from 4.1% in July. That would translate into a trade surplus of $101.4 billion. China's customs bureau is scheduled to release the data on Monday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

0929 GMT - China's foreign-exchange reserves likely rose by $18 billion to $3.31 trillion at the end of August, according to a poll of economists by The Wall Street Journal. A rise would reverse the decline recorded in July that put an end to six straight months of increases. The yuan appreciated against the U.S. dollar in August, after losing ground against the dollar in July. The People's Bank of China is set to release the data on Sunday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

0925 GMT - Singapore's retail sales strengthened in July, though UOB warns the momentum may not last as the labor market shows signs of cooling. Retail receipts rose 4.8% from a year ago. However, polls conducted by Singapore's manpower ministry showed fewer firms plan to hire or raise salaries in 3Q, while more companies intend to freeze wages, says associate economist Jester Koh in a note. This points to slower wage growth for the rest of 2025 and possibly into 2026, which likely dampens discretionary spending, he says. Even so, upcoming events such as October's Formula 1 race could lend support to retail activity, Koh adds. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

0910 GMT - Sterling could weaken in the long run as U.K. Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner's future looks uncertain after admitting she underpaid stamp duty on an apartment, Monex Europe analysts say in a note. An independent ethics advisor is set to report on whether Rayner broke ministerial standard rules later. "We think the near-term consequences for sterling are limited, albeit the longer-term implications are not," the analysts say. Recent events have undermined the government's credibility, which could weigh on sterling even if it's not an immediate catalyst, they say. Sterling rises 0.2% to $1.1365 against a stronger dollar but falls versus the euro, which is up 0.1% at 0.8672 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0907 GMT - U.K. retail sales are expected to decelerate in the coming months as the labor market weakens and household incomes decline, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. "Declines in payroll employment and easing real wage growth will squeeze household finances over the winter," he says. Concerns about potential tax rises at the autumn budget could also limit consumer spending, Wishart says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0852 GMT - Minimum hourly wages in Japan's 47 prefectures will average 1,121 yen in the current fiscal year ending March 2026, a record increase of 66 yen. That is an encouraging sign for the virtuous cycle of wages and prices, which policymakers expect to work more smoothly. Persistent inflation has made Japanese consumers hesitant to spend actively. Private consumption in July is likely to have remained largely flat from the previous month, says Daiwa Institute of Research economist Yoshiya Kikuchi. The rise in minimum wages, as well as solid summer bonuses, will likely help a recovery in spending toward the end of the year, he adds. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0842 GMT - Friday's U.S. nonfarm payroll data for August will be crucial for expectations for future interest-rate cuts, says TwentyFour Asset Management's Felipe Villarroel in a note. The data will be key not just for the path of short-term rates but also for shaping perceptions of political bias in Federal Reserve officials' decision-making, he says. Longer-dated bonds "would react negatively to the perception of political influence at the Fed," he says. U.S. Treasury yields fall by up to 1.5 basis points, with the 10-year yield easing 1 basis point to 4.165%, according to Tradeweb. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal poll expect 75,000 jobs were added in August. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0840 GMT - The European Central Bank is expected to leave interest rates on hold next Thursday and the market impact is likely to be limited, says Mediolanum's Niall Scanlon in a note. "We don't expect this meeting to be a market mover, as consensus points to a hold in rates from the ECB next week," the fixed income portfolio manager says. Economic data after the meeting will be more significant. Mediolanum expects data could weaken in the coming months as tariffs weighs on growth and German fiscal stimulus takes a while to feed through. Headline inflation is also forecast to be below target in 2026. The ECB could consider cutting rates again towards the end of 2025 or early in 2026, Scanlon says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0826 GMT - A much weaker-than-expected U.S. nonfarm payrolls report at 1230 GMT would have a bigger dollar impact than much stronger-than-forecast data, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. A major payrolls miss should prompt markets to price in three Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts by year-end, he says. The market is fully priced for two cuts, LSEG data show. A major upside surprise could be treated with "much more caution as markets might start to doubt the credibility of data under the new leadership at the Bureau of Labor Statistics." President Trump recently nominated conservative economist EJ Antoni to replace BLS head Erika McEntarfer. The DXY dollar index falls 0.3% to 98.055. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: S&P 500 futures hit record high ahead of payrolls data; Broadcom surges
data.description:
Key points:
  • Futures: Dow flat, S&P 500 up 0.23%, Nasdaq 0.49%

Futures tied to the S&P 500 touched record highs and Nasdaq futures edged upward ahead of key nonfarm payrolls data on Friday, with chip major Broadcom's quarterly earnings adding to gains.

Broadcom jumped 9% before the bell after the chip designer forecast fourth-quarter revenue above estimates and expected AI revenue growth to "improve significantly" in fiscal-year 2026.

The jobs report for August is the last major labor market dataset expected to be released ahead of the September 16 and 17 U.S. Federal Reserve meeting.

Markets have priced in a more than 99% chance of a 25-basis-point cut, according to CME's FedWatch Tool, after weak July payrolls as well as Fed Chair Jerome Powell's dovish comments in August.

Labor market data released this week reinforced the view that the U.S. jobs market was weakening.

"Labor markets aren't collapsing, but they aren't reaccelerating either... instead, the trend of linear cooling continues as the labor market remains in stasis," said Garrett Melson, portfolio strategist at Natixis IM Solutions.

"While the 25- versus 50-bps debate is rippling up again, barring a massively negative headline payrolls print paired with a surge in the unemployment rate, 50 bps at the September meeting seems a bridge too far."

At 05:05 a.m. ET, Dow E-minis (YMcv1) were down 4 points, or 0.01%, S&P 500 E-minis rose 15 points, or 0.23%, and Nasdaq 100 E-minis gained 115.75 points, or 0.49%.

Investors, who were unimpressed by Nvidia's quarterly results and concerned after the lofty valuations of AI-related firms briefly paused Wall Street's rally last month, welcomed Broadcom's results.

Conversely, Lululemon Athletica plunged 17.3% after the yogawear maker slashed its annual profit forecast the second time in a row, dragging larger rival Nike down 1.1%.

Despite the anticipation around the payrolls, the S&P 500 closed at a record high on Thursday, with the Nasdaq and the Dow also ending higher as rate-cut hopes remained intact after a slew of economic data.

The benchmark index is within touching distance of an intraday record high set on August 28.

Wall Street's three main indexes were on track for gains in the first week of September. The S&P 500 has lost 1.5% on average in September since 2000, according to LSEG data, making it the worst month historically for U.S. equities.

In other moves, Samsara advanced 9.7% after the software firm beat second-quarter revenue expectations.

Cryptocurrency stocks rose as bitcoin prices hit a more than one-week high. Strategy was up 2.3%, Coinbase added 1.9% and miner Riot Platforms climbed 2%.

data.title: India regulator ramped up Jane Street probe due to inadequate data, continued complaints, sources say
data.description:
India's markets regulator launched a formal investigation into Jane Street's trading practices even though its surveillance department had recommended otherwise, due to continued complaints from market participants, two sources with direct knowledge of the matter said.

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) also believed inadequate data was used in the initial probe into the U.S. high-frequency trading firm, the people said, amid fears it might have manipulated the country's stock and bond markets.

SEBI on July 4 temporarily barred the firm from local markets on allegations of market manipulation, which the company has denied.

SEBI imposed a $567 million penalty on Jane Street, which the company has deposited. While it can restart trading in India, it has refrained from doing so, Reuters reported last month.

On Wednesday, Jane Street filed an appeal before the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) against SEBI seeking documents and data which led to the formal inquiry.

The firm questioned why the regulator went against the advice of its own surveillance department that the inquiry against Jane Street should be closed and sought documents that supported the regulator's change of stance.

The appeal will be heard on Monday by the Tribunal.

Emails sent to SEBI and Jane Street seeking comments were not answered. The firm had earlier declined to comment on its appeal.

According to the two sources, SEBI's top leadership was not satisfied with the robustness of the first examination concluded by its own surveillance department on December 11 and chose to initiate a formal investigation towards the end of December 2024, which gives it powers to seek data from the trading firm's custodian bank and domestic trading partner.

A formal investigation is a quasi-legal process under Indian regulatory rules, in contrast to an internal enquiry.

According to Sumit Agrawal, a former SEBI official and founding partner of Regstreet Law Advisor, once a formal investigation is initiated any prior conclusions lose their weight, and the process begins anew.

"Earlier determinations, whether favorable or adverse, are set aside in favor of an independent investigation," he said.

The regulator had also continued to receive complaints from market participants of manipulation of India’s key indexes, the two people said.

The firm in its appeal has sought copies of these complaints.

One such complaint was filed by UAE-based options trader Mayank Bansal on December 17, who told Reuters that "communication between a market participant and the regulator is premised on confidentiality".

The department which oversees India's market regulation later that month recommended opening an investigation to bring finality to the matter, the first source said.

SEBI tasked a new team with reviewing the firm's trading activity over a much longer time frame than the trading data examined by its surveillance department. The data was also much more detailed, the two people said.

While the regulator continued the investigation it issued a warning to the firm through Indian exchanges in February that it should refrain from taking large positions on days when derivatives contracts expire, according to regulator's 4 July order. Price volatility can often spike around those expirations.

But the firm's trading performance on May 15 which earned it 3.7 billion rupees ($42.28 million) forced SEBI's hand to pass an order, said the first source.

"SEBI officers worked overnight to finish its investigation in June, which ultimately led to passing the order," the first person said.

($1 = 87.5060 Indian rupees)

data.title: European Credit Default Swaps Fall Ahead of U.S. Jobs Data — Market Talk
data.description: The cost of insuring euro credit against default falls due to improved sentiment on prospects of weak U.S. jobs data causing the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates. The U.S. non-farm payrolls data due to be released at 1230 GMT is expected to show weakness in the labor market, raising the possibility of several interest rate cuts by the Fed for the remainder of 2025. The job openings data and ADP jobs data released this week came in weaker-than-expected, and it will be no surprise if the non-farm payrolls data is also weak, Rostro's Joshua Mahony says in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index, which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps, declines 2 basis points to last trade at 261bps, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Memcyco Wins Across 3 Categories in Cybersecurity Excellence Awards
data.description:
The Cybersecurity Excellence Awards are now in their tenth year of honoring companies and products that demonstrate innovation and impact in cybersecurity. This recognition highlights Memcyco's growth and the strength of its preemptive approach to fraud protection, which moves beyond traditional detection and takedown models to expose and disrupt attacks right from their inception, and as they unfold.

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / September 5, 2025 / Memcyco, the innovator of real-time, preemptive fraud defense solutions that disrupt digital impersonation and account takeover (ATO) fraud, today announced that it has been named a winner in the 2025 Cybersecurity Excellence Awards across three categories. Memcyco earned recognition in the Fraud Prevention, Most Innovative Cybersecurity Company, and Best Cybersecurity Industry Solution categories, underscoring the company's leadership in protecting enterprises and their customers against phishing, digital impersonation, and ATO attacks.

The Cybersecurity Excellence Awards are now in their tenth year of honoring companies and products that demonstrate innovation and impact in cybersecurity. This recognition highlights Memcyco's growth and the strength of its preemptive approach to fraud protection, which moves beyond traditional detection and takedown models to expose and disrupt attacks right from their inception, and as they unfold.

"We congratulate Memcyco on these outstanding achievements across the Fraud Prevention, Most Innovative Cybersecurity Company, and Best Cybersecurity Industry Solution categories of the 2025 Cybersecurity Excellence Awards," said Holger Schulze, founder of Cybersecurity Insiders and organizer of the Cybersecurity Excellence Awards. "As we celebrate 10 years of recognizing excellence in cybersecurity, Memcyco's innovation, commitment, and leadership set a powerful example for the entire industry."

Memcyco's PoSA™ solution provides real-time visibility, protection, and attack disruption by:

  • Infiltration of the attack timeline: anticipating and preempting ATO attempts before damage occurs.

  • Deception-driven disruption: deploying decoy data and running deception campaigns to mislead attackers and neutralize phishing, MitM, and SEO poisoning threats.

  • Victim-level visibility: identifying exactly which customers are targeted, enabling precise and timely response.

"Recognition from the Cybersecurity Excellence Awards validates our mission to redefine digital fraud defense," said Israel Mazin, CEO at Memcyco. "Fraud evolves too quickly for reactive methods. Our preemptive technology protects customer trust in real time, stopping attacks before they impact users or brand reputation."

This award adds to Memcyco's growing list of industry accolades, including recognition from Datos Insights in its Q1 2025 Fintech Spotlight report, and at the Global InfoSec Awards at RSA, Globee Awards, and Cyber 150 two years in a row. The company continues to expand its role as a trusted partner to financial institutions, retailers, airlines, higher education institutions, and enterprises worldwide.

About Cybersecurity Excellence Awards

The Cybersecurity Excellence Awards celebrate organizations, products, and professionals that demonstrate innovation and leadership in information security. Now in their tenth year, the awards are organized by Cybersecurity Insiders and recognize companies addressing the most pressing cyber risks with proven solutions and measurable results. Honorees represent excellence across key domains, including fraud prevention, threat detection, cloud security, AI-driven defense, and digital trust. Winning a Cybersecurity Excellence Award signals industry recognition, peer validation, and a commitment to advancing global cybersecurity resilience.

About Memcyco

Memcyco delivers real-time, preemptive protection to companies and their customers against AI-driven phishing, digital impersonation, and account takeover (ATO) fraud. By infiltrating the attack timeline as it unfolds, Memcyco provides unmatched visibility and control, enabling organizations to identify individual victims, predict incidents, and disrupt attacks before damage occurs. With its agentless deployment and rapid time to value, Memcyco reduces fraud-related losses, strengthens compliance readiness, and delivers measurable ROI while safeguarding digital trust.

Contact: Sheena Kretzmer

Title: Digital Marketing Director

Email: sheena@memcyco.com

SOURCE: Memcyco LTD

data.title: DOMO, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
DOMO, INC., a leading provider of cloud-based business intelligence and data visualization solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report highlights the company's financial and operational performance, showcasing stable revenue growth, improved operational efficiency, and strategic business initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $79.7 million, reflecting a slight increase from $78.4 million in the same period last year, indicating stable sales performance.
  • Gross Profit: $59.6 million, showing an improvement from $58.3 million, highlighting effective cost management in revenue generation.
  • Loss from Operations: $(7.3) million, a reduction in operating loss compared to $(14.5) million, demonstrating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Loss: $(22.9) million, compared to $(19.5) million, indicating a slight increase in net loss due to higher other expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share, Basic and Diluted: $(0.56), compared to $(0.51), reflecting the impact of increased net loss on a per-share basis.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company primarily offers its platform as a consumption-based service, which includes consumption-based agreements and enterprise-wide agreements (ELAs) with unlimited users and a data cap. As of the end of the most recent fiscal quarter, 76% of the annual recurring revenue (ARR) was utilizing the platform as a consumption-based service.
  • Geographical Performance: Revenue from customers with billing addresses in the United States comprised 80% of the total revenue for both the three and six months ended July 31, 2024 and 2025. No other individual country exceeded 10% of total revenue.
  • Customer Segments: As of July 31, 2025, the company had over 2,500 customers, with enterprise customers accounting for 44% of revenue for both the three and six months ended July 31, 2024 and 2025.
  • Customer Retention: The company's trailing twelve-month gross retention rate was 85% as of July 31, 2025, indicating a slight improvement from 84% as of July 31, 2024.
  • Sales and Marketing Efficiency: Sales and marketing expenses as a percentage of total revenue decreased from 47% for the three months ended July 31, 2024, to 44% for the three months ended July 31, 2025, reflecting improved efficiency.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects the percentage of ARR utilizing the platform as a consumption-based service to increase in future periods. Additionally, the company is focused on aligning its sales team and controlling costs to improve margins, sustain positive cash flow, and achieve efficient growth in the long term.

SEC Filing: DOMO, INC. [ DOMO ] - 10-Q - Sep. 04, 2025

data.title: Broadcom Stock Rises on New AI Chip Customer Order After Earnings Beat — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Tae Kim and Adam Clark

Broadcom stock surged after the company announced on its earnings call that it has won an order from a new customer.

For its fiscal third quarter, the company reported revenue of $15.95 billion versus the Wall Street consensus of $15.8 billion, according to FactSet. Adjusted earnings came in at $1.69 per share, which is above analysts' consensus estimate of $1.66 per share.

Guidance was solid. For the current quarter, Broadcom guided to $17.4 billion in revenue, compared to analysts' expectations of $17 billion.

"Broadcom achieved record third quarter revenue on continued strength in custom AI accelerators, networking and VMware," Broadcom CEO Hock Tan said in the release.

Shares of Broadcom rose 9.1% to $334.21 in premarket trading.

On the earnings call, Tan announced Broadcom had secured $10 billion in AI orders from a new qualified customer, which he believes will "significantly" improve the AI revenue outlook for fiscal 2026.

Broadcom shares wavered up and down in after-hours trading following the release, and then rose 3% after Tan announced the new unnamed customer win.

Multiple Wall Street analysts said they believe the new customer is ChatGPT-developer OpenAI, which didn't immediately respond to a Barron's request for comment.

"We're impressed by the addition of a fourth AI customer and remain Overweight," wrote KeyBanc analyst John Vinh, who increased his target price on the stock to $400 from $330.

Broadcom's semiconductors compete in several categories, including networking, broadband, server storage, wireless, and industrial. The company is also a leader in the market for high-end artificial intelligence application-specific integrated circuits, or AI ASICs. It helps large technology companies design custom chips for AI.

Write to Tae Kim at tae.kim@barrons.com and Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Braze, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report

Braze, Inc., a leading customer engagement platform, has released its Form 10-Q report for the three months ended July 31, 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational performance metrics, showcasing the company's continued growth and strategic initiatives in the customer engagement industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $180.1 million, representing a year-over-year growth of 23.8% for the three months ended July 31, 2025.
  • Gross Profit: $121.9 million, reflecting the company's ability to maintain a strong margin despite increased costs.
  • Loss from Operations: $(38.8) million, indicating a higher operating loss compared to the previous year due to increased operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(27.8) million, compared to $(23.1) million in the prior year, highlighting the impact of increased expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.26), showing a slight increase in loss per share from the previous year's $(0.23).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Braze, Inc. generates revenue primarily from subscription services and professional services. Subscription services include access to the customer engagement platform, while professional services help customers onboard and use the platform effectively.
  • Geographical Performance: Approximately 45% of Braze's revenue for the six months ended July 31, 2025, was generated outside of the United States, with a focus on increasing market penetration in Europe, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America.
  • Customer Base Expansion: As of July 31, 2025, Braze had 2,422 customers across various industries, with a strategy to expand in verticals like retail, media, telecommunications, and financial services.
  • Monthly Active Users: The company reported approximately 7.4 billion monthly active users as of July 31, 2025, up from 7.2 billion as of January 31, 2025, indicating growth in user engagement.
  • Dollar-Based Net Retention Rate: The dollar-based net retention rate for the trailing 12 months ended July 31, 2025, was 108% for all customers and 111% for customers with ARR of $500,000 or more, reflecting customer retention and expansion.
  • Future Outlook: Braze plans to continue investing in research and development to enhance its platform, focusing on artificial intelligence capabilities and expanding channel offerings to drive future growth.
  • Macroeconomic Impact: The company acknowledges that macroeconomic conditions, such as inflation and interest rate changes, may impact customer purchasing decisions and overall business performance.

data.title: Major Japanese copper smelter to cut output, capacity as margins erode
data.description:
Key points:
  • Cutting FY25 output by tens of thousands of tons from earlier plans
  • Will unveil long-term plans to shrink smelting capacity
  • Seeking to invest in upstream projects to secure rare metals

JX Advanced Metals will likely cut copper production by tens of thousands of metric tons in fiscal 2025 compared with earlier plans and unveil a roadmap to reduce smelting capacity by March, President Yoichi Hayashi said, as shrinking fees erode margins.

Japanese copper smelters are grappling with tumbling treatment and refining charges (TC/RCs) and shrinking smelting margins due to a shortage of concentrate supply and growing smelting capacity in China. In June, some Chinese smelters agreed to process copper for Chilean miner Antofagasta at no charge.

"In the short term, we plan to slash annual electrolytic copper output by several tens of thousands of tons from our earlier estimate as we can't purchase concentrates under current conditions," Hayashi told Reuters this week.

JX, one of Japan's top copper smelters with 450,000 tons of annual production capacity, warned in June that cuts were possible. Its fiscal year ends in March.

Longer term, it intends to shrink capacity to reduce risks in concentrate procurement and smelting, Hayashi said, though he gave no scale.

Roughly half of JX's refined copper is exported, mainly to China, where demand could weaken as new domestic smelters ramp up. Rival Mitsubishi Materials is also weighing reductions.

Asked if lower copper output would affect domestic industries, Hayashi said there would be no immediate impact, as only half is used domestically. If more capacity was needed, the government should provide support, he said.

Despite cutbacks, Hayashi stressed that smelting remains essential for recovering rare metals, such as tantalum, critical to semiconductor materials, a key growth business.

"We are reviewing the optimal scale from various perspectives, including the use of recycling materials," he said.

Mid-year negotiations between Japanese smelters and global miners broke off without a TC/RC settlement, forcing firms to skip contracted term supplies, industry sources said.

Hayashi said some miners are willing to negotiate different terms with Japanese firms from the Chinese benchmark deals to help sustain the world's fourth-largest smelting sector.

JX is accelerating its pivot away from mining and smelting toward semiconductor materials. In June, it said it would acquire a stake in the Copi mineral sands project in Australia, led by RZ Resources (RRZ.AX), to secure rare metals used in chip materials.

"We are actively seeking new projects," Hayashi said, adding that future upstream deals would be far smaller than its past Chilean copper mine investment, which resulted in heavy impairment losses.

Since its March listing, JX has streamlined decision-making and enhanced investor engagement. It recently raised its full-year earnings forecast, with Hayashi expressing confidence in achieving fiscal 2027 goals, including an operating profit margin of 12% to 17%.

By 2040, JX aims to more than double operating profit to 250 billion yen ($1.7 billion).

"Achieving our 2040 goal will require not only organic growth but also large-scale M&A starting in fiscal 2028," Hayashi said.

data.title: Protesters picnic outside Indonesian parliament on national holiday
data.description:
There were small, peaceful protests outside the Indonesian parliament on Friday afternoon, following more than a week of sometimes violent demonstrations, as the Muslim-majority country celebrated a holiday marking the Prophet Mohammad's birthday.

Students gathered for a "picnic protest", with poetry and book readings as they criticised politicians' benefits and called for release of detained protesters and a withdrawal of military personnel from civil security duty.

"We have a picnic together, show our expression creatively, innovatively ... we don't just shout angrily, but we have a cool, creative approach to express our anger," Vincent Thomas, a student leader from Padjadjaran University, said in a speech broadcast on Kompas TV.

Last week, protesters including students, workers and rights groups, took to the streets of Jakarta to protest against MPs' housing allowances. Unrest spread across the country after a police vehicle hit and killed a motorcycle taxi driver at one rally.

Jakarta police spokesperson Ade Ary Syam Indradi told reporters 1,371 personnel, including from the police and the military, were deployed across the capital on Friday.

In Solo, central Java, more than 500 km (310 miles) east of Jakarta, students handed out groceries and conducted free health screenings as part of their peaceful protest, Kompas reported.

On Thursday, student groups met cabinet ministers to press their complaints over lawmakers' perks and police tactics used against demonstrators.

Student representatives also met with some parliamentarians earlier in the week, but have so far not been successful in their demands for a meeting with President Prabowo Subianto.

Rights groups say 10 people have died and more than 1,000 have been injured in clashes with security forces and other unrest.

Authorities have detained more than 3,000 people in a nationwide crackdown on the protests, the New York-based rights group Human Rights Watch said.

data.title: REG - Thames Wtr Util. Ltd - Fourth Consent Requests - Super Senior Funding
data.description:
RNS Number : 2330Y Thames Water Utilities Limited 05 September 2025  

Thames Water Utilities Limited

5 September 2025

Fourth Consent Requests relating to Super Senior Issuer Funding

Background

On 27 August 2025, Thames Water Utilities Limited ("TWUL") and Ofwat announced the agreement of scheduled payments ("Payment Plan") in relation to the penalty notices issued by Ofwat to TWUL on 28 May 2025 (the "Penalty Notices"). TWUL's announcement in respect of the Payment Plan can be found at the following link:

https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/AW14/investigations-update-payment-plan/17201405

Fourth Consent Requests

Prior to making the first payment scheduled under the Payment Plan, TWUL and its subsidiary, Thames Water Super Senior Issuer PLC (the "Super Senior Issuer"), have today launched a fourth set of consent requests (the "Fourth Consent Requests") seeking the consent of the super senior creditors to designate the Payment Plan as an approved payment plan under the super senior liquidity facility (the "Facility") entered into between TWUL and the Super Senior Issuer.

The Fourth Consent Requests have a voting date of 19 September 2025 and follow three previous sets of consent requests which were approved by creditors in April, May and July 2025.

TWUL has so far drawn £872m of the initial £1.5bn available under the Facility.

TWUL continues to work closely with stakeholders to secure a market led solution.

Access to Documents

The Fourth Consent Requests are available through TWUL's investor relations website at https://www.thameswater.co.uk/about-us/investors/liquidity-extension-update.

Notes for Editors

Glencore is one of the world's largest global diversified natural resource companies and a major producer and marketer of more than 60 commodities that advance everyday life. Through a network of assets, customers and suppliers that spans the globe, we produce, process, recycle, source, market and distribute the commodities that support decarbonisation while meeting the energy needs of today.

With over 150,000 employees and contractors and a strong footprint in over 30 countries in both established and emerging regions for natural resources, our marketing and industrial activities are supported by a global network of more than 50 offices.

Glencore's customers are industrial consumers, such as those in the automotive, steel, power generation, battery manufacturing and oil sectors. We also provide financing, logistics and other services to producers and consumers of commodities.

Glencore is proud to be a member of the Voluntary Principles on Security and Human Rights and the International Council on Mining and Metals. We are an active participant in the Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative.

We will support the global effort to achieve the goals of the Paris Agreement through our efforts to decarbonise our own operational footprint. For more information see our 2024-2026 Climate Action Transition Plan, available on our website at glencore.com/publications.
